axis: PH (policy heterogeneity) — different partner POLICY, same Panda embodiment
purpose: Pre-register the Rung-3 policy-heterogeneity measurement BEFORE any reference/shifted episode is run, so the result is honest (no forking paths). The frozen, held-out-validated Rung-2 incumbent (freeze manifest, commit 802df94) is held fixed and paired with capability-matched policy-shift teammates (same Panda body, materially different control policies); the question is whether a different partner POLICY degrades cooperation relative to the matched reference. Embodiment is held fixed (the xArm6 teammate is Rung 4). Mirrors the Rung-2 eval-only freeze pre-registration (commit 5fc291b): the teammate set, C_min, Δ_min, seeds, decision rule, pooling, and null rule are fixed and committed here, and the measurement is computed only AFTER this file is committed + git-tagged.

governance.phase: Phase-2 (the Phase-1/2 scope question is an open founder decision; it does not block running this)
governance.black_box_partner_I3_ADR009: teammates enter only through the partner interface; they read task leaves (goal_pos / bar_pose / their own qpos) only, never the ego's identity or internals
governance.no_schema_bump_I9: reuses the existing serialisation surface with a new co-carry tag (cocarry_rung3_ph_prereg/v1); no bump to any *.SCHEMA_VERSION / PREREG_SCHEMA_VERSION
governance.do_not_merge: STOP at the open PR for founder review

unchanged_bar.success_predicate: is_placed & is_level & is_unstressed & both_static & is_settled (cocarry.py evaluate()); UNCHANGED
unchanged_bar.note: The matched reference (frozen incumbent + matched cocarry_impedance partner) is the cooperation ceiling. The success predicate, f_max, and the 0.10 m radius are NOT moved for this slice.

capability_gate.rule: A candidate enters the PH test iff, paired with the cooperative-reference ego (matched cocarry_impedance on the ego seat — NOT the frozen incumbent), its joint success rate over the calibration seeds is >= C_min. Defuses the weaker-teammate confound. A candidate that cannot be capability-matched is EXCLUDED, and the exclusion is archived.
capability_gate.c_min_derivation: Grounded on the matched cooperative reference (~1.0 joint success; Rung-1 matched competence, Rung-2 24/24 on held-out V). C_min = max(0.75, M - 0.25) with the matched teammate's calibrated score M; M = 1.0 (measured in the roster) gives C_min = 0.75 — a candidate must jointly succeed with the proven cooperative controller on >= 9/12 calibration seeds. The 0.25 margin admits legitimately-different-but-competent policies (so the gate does not truncate the heterogeneity it is meant to admit) while excluding teammates that simply cannot do the task. Fixed at pre-statement; NEVER weakened to pass teammates.

teammate_policy_descriptions.cocarry_stiff_impedance: stiffness shift: high-gain, low-damping, no-integral fast tracking (transmits more interaction force, overshoots more than the compliant matched controller)
teammate_policy_descriptions.cocarry_admittance: compliance shift: a force-follower that mostly holds station at its current bar end and contributes only a gentle goal-seeking bias, letting the partner arm lead
teammate_policy_descriptions.cocarry_slew_impedance: trajectory-timing shift: matched gains but the target is slewed along a rate-limited ramp over a lead-in (same destination, different timing)

excluded_roster.cocarry_nullspace_impedance: EXCLUDED at calibration: redundancy-resolution shift (null-space posture term) pulled the arm off the cooperative target — calibration success 0/12, centroid p50 0.146 m (> 0.10 radius, fails placed 12/12) and stress p90 184 N (> 130, fails unstressed 12/12). Not capability-matched (a weaker teammate the gate is designed to catch). Archived; NOT entered into the test.

delta_min_basis.rationale: 0.20 is a meaningful cooperation drop (a fifth of episodes lost to the policy shift). The archived power simulation over the ACTUAL decision procedure (cluster bootstrap one-sided CI + the decision rule) shows the rule is conservative under the null (type-I ~0 at Δ=0) and resolves Δ_min=0.20 (power ~0.45 exactly at the mean-threshold boundary, ~0.86 at Δ=0.30). n (12 clusters) is the precision lever, NOT the effect-size lever (fewer clusters are exploration only).

measurement_design: Paired within-seed: the SAME 12 measurement seeds drive the reference (frozen incumbent + matched cocarry_impedance partner) and each shifted condition (frozen incumbent + each admitted teammate). One deterministic episode per seed (single-env; the only within-seed variation is the goal jitter). The reference is reconfirmed first; if it does not reach >= 0.90 the run STOPs (something changed) before the shifted arm is trusted.
pooling_method: Pooled Δ = reference_rate - mean_over_teammates(shifted_rate), each admitted teammate weighted equally (the pooled estimate the decision rule + power sim use). Per-teammate Δ is also reported. The cluster (seed) bootstrap resamples the 12 paired seed-clusters with replacement and recomputes the pooled Δ each iteration (10000 iterations; RNG via derive_substream, P6).

decision_rule.primary: Conclude 'policy heterogeneity reduces cooperation' (verdict ph_reduces_cooperation) iff the pooled mean Δ >= Δ_min (0.20) AND the one-sided 95% CI on the pooled Δ excludes 0 (CI lower bound > 0).
decision_rule.secondary_iqm: IQM (25%-trimmed mean) of the per-seed success indicators, reference and pooled-shifted, is reported as SECONDARY and is NOT gate-bearing.
decision_rule.confirmatory_glmm: A cluster-robust binomial GLM (statsmodels GEE, binomial family, exchangeable working correlation, clustered by seed): success ~ shifted. A significantly negative shifted coefficient confirms the bootstrap drop. Confirmatory, not the headline.
decision_rule.null_rule: The coupling positive-control passes (single-arm ~ 0, established at Rung 1) AND Δ ~ 0 (the one-sided 95% CI excludes Δ_min, i.e. the two-sided upper bound < Δ_min) ⇒ a thesis-disconfirming null: no policy-heterogeneity penalty on this axis under this task. Reported as such — NEVER re-described as a coupling failure.
decision_rule.indeterminate: Neither the drop nor the null rule met ⇒ indeterminate (collect more seed-clusters or sharpen the teammate set; decision-relevant either way).

null_caveat: EXCLUSIONS OCCURRED (cocarry_nullspace_impedance). The capability gate truncates heterogeneity from above (it excludes teammates the cooperative reference cannot succeed with), biasing Δ toward zero. If the verdict is a null, it may NOT be read as 'the PH axis does not couple to cooperation' — only as 'among capability-matched policies, no qualifying drop was observed'.
conjunct_reporting: On drops, the per-conjunct failure breakdown (placed / level / unstressed / static) is reported so the cooperation channel the shifted partner tips over is visible. The reference lands at centroid ~0.099 m vs the 0.10 m radius, so transport precision is the likeliest channel a shifted partner tips over — a legitimate cooperative channel, surfaced not assumed.
honesty_statement: As of this committed file, NO reference or shifted episode on the measurement seeds (30000-30011) has been evaluated on the frozen incumbent. Only the capability calibration (seeds 40000-40011, cooperative reference, NOT the incumbent) and a synthetic power simulation (no incumbent) have been run. The reference + shifted measurement is computed only AFTER this pre-registration is committed and git-tagged.
repro_commands: bash scripts/repro/cocarry_rung3_calibrate.sh   # DONE — produced the calibration roster
git add + commit + tag prereg-cocarry-rung3-ph-2026-06-19  # this file, BEFORE measuring
bash scripts/repro/cocarry_rung3_ph_measure.sh   # reference + shifted, Δ + CI + GLMM + verdict
